current.task: Build let-float: float lets outward for optimization. 10+ tests.

blocks.task: Evening C: Review Session B accomplishments (1882+ tests!). Pick 1-2 evening goals. Bias toward EXPLORE/depth.
blocks.summary: T2: Lambda Calculus interpreter - new mini-project.
blocks.details: T2: Lambda Calculus interpreter - new mini-project. 2 BUILD tasks.

blocks.task: Evening exploration + depth work
blocks.summary: PLAN: Lambda Calculus interpreter - pure exploration project. Parser, evaluator, Church encodings, Y combinator, de Bruijn indices. 2 BUILD tasks.
blocks.details: PLAN: Lambda Calculus interpreter - pure exploration project. Parser, evaluator, Church encodings, Y combinator, de Bruijn indices. 2 BUILD tasks.

blocks.task: Build a Lambda Calculus interpreter from scratch - parser, evaluator, Church encodings, Y combinator, de Bruijn indices, normal-order reduction
blocks.summary: Lambda Calculus interpreter from scratch: tokenizer, parser (multi-param, left-assoc app), capture-avoiding substitution, 4 reduction strategies (normal/applicative/CBV/CBN), de Bruijn indices, alpha-equivalence, Church encodings (booleans, numerals, pairs, lists), Y/Z combinators, step tracing. 104 tests, 0 failures.
blocks.details: Lambda Calculus interpreter from scratch: tokenizer, parser (multi-param, left-assoc app), capture-avoiding substitution, 4 reduction strategies (normal/applicative/CBV/CBN), de Bruijn indices, alpha-equivalence, Church encodings (booleans, numerals, pairs, lists), Y/Z combinators, step tracing. 104 tests, 0 failures.

blocks.task: Write stress tests and explore computability: factorial, fibonacci, pairs, lists all in pure lambda calculus
blocks.summary: T3: Lambda Calculus interpreter from scratch.
blocks.details: T3: Lambda Calculus interpreter from scratch. Tokenizer, parser (multi-param, left-assoc app), capture-avoiding substitution, 4 reduction strategies (normal/applicative/CBV/CBN), de Bruijn indices, alpha-equivalence, Church encodings (booleans, numerals, pairs, lists), Y/Z combinators, step tracing. 104 tests, 0 failures.

blocks.task: Housekeeping: git push, knowledge capture
blocks.summary: T6: Computability tests.
blocks.details: T6: Computability tests. Fibonacci via Church pairs, SKI combinators (I=SKK, composition B=S(KS)K), Scott-encoded naturals, boolean logic completeness (XOR/NAND/IMPLIES), divergence detection, stress tests (exp 2^5=32), Y/Turing fixed-point. Found Church exp 0-exponent quirk. 55 new tests, 159 total.

blocks.task: Evening curiosity time
blocks.summary: T4: Git push, dashboard check, knowledge capture (lambda-calculus-quirks.md).
blocks.details: T4: Git push, dashboard check, knowledge capture (lambda-calculus-quirks.md).

blocks.task: Lambda calculus depth: what is the hardest reduction case? Can we build a simply typed lambda calculus?
blocks.summary: THINK: Chose Simply Typed Lambda Calculus (STLC) as next extension. Type checker + strong normalization. Connects to type-inference project.
blocks.details: THINK: Chose Simply Typed Lambda Calculus (STLC) as next extension. Type checker + strong normalization. Connects to type-inference project.

blocks.task: Build simply typed lambda calculus with type checker
blocks.summary: PLAN: 2 BUILD tasks — STLC type system with bidirectional checking, then CPS transformation.
blocks.details: PLAN: 2 BUILD tasks — STLC type system with bidirectional checking, then CPS transformation.

blocks.task: Build STLC: type AST (Bool, Int, Arrow), typed terms, bidirectional type checker, type environments, let-bindings, if-then-else. Tests for well-typed and ill-typed terms.
blocks.summary: Simply Typed Lambda Calculus: types (Bool, Int, Unit, Arrow, Product), bidirectional type checker (infer/check), CBV evaluator, let-bindings, if-then-else, binary ops, pairs (fst/snd), fix-point recursion. Programs: factorial, fibonacci, GCD, sum 1..n. Strong normalization proof for STLC-without-fix. 82 new tests, 290 total across all modules.
blocks.details: Simply Typed Lambda Calculus: types (Bool, Int, Unit, Arrow, Product), bidirectional type checker (infer/check), CBV evaluator, let-bindings, if-then-else, binary ops, pairs (fst/snd), fix-point recursion. Programs: factorial, fibonacci, GCD, sum 1..n. Strong normalization proof for STLC-without-fix. 82 new tests, 290 total across all modules.

blocks.task: CPS transformation: compile lambda calculus to continuation-passing style. A-normal form. Test CPS equivalence with direct evaluation.
blocks.summary: T10: CPS transformation: Fischer/Plotkin CPS, Danvy-Filinski one-pass, ANF.
blocks.details: T10: CPS transformation: Fischer/Plotkin CPS, Danvy-Filinski one-pass, ANF. 33 new tests, 323 total.

blocks.task: Final housekeeping + git push
blocks.summary: Git pushed, state clean.
blocks.details: Git pushed, state clean.

blocks.task: Lambda calculus project is 323 tests with 5 modules. What depth check: am I in a breadth spiral? What would genuinely surprise me?
blocks.summary: Depth check: 2/3 positive (Church exp quirk, Danvy-Filinski technique). NOT breadth spiral. Direction: System F (polymorphic lambda calculus) - genuinely hard, theoretical foundation for generics.
blocks.details: Depth check: 2/3 positive (Church exp quirk, Danvy-Filinski technique). NOT breadth spiral. Direction: System F (polymorphic lambda calculus) - genuinely hard, theoretical foundation for generics.

blocks.task: System F: polymorphic lambda calculus with type abstraction/application
blocks.summary: PLAN: System F (polymorphic lambda calculus). 2 BUILD tasks: core implementation + stress tests.
blocks.details: PLAN: System F (polymorphic lambda calculus). 2 BUILD tasks: core implementation + stress tests.

blocks.task: Build System F: type variables, universal quantification (forall), type abstraction (Λα.t), type application (t[T]), type checker, evaluator. Classic examples: polymorphic identity, Church booleans, existential types via encoding.
blocks.summary: System F (polymorphic lambda calculus): type variables, ∀-quantification, type abstraction (Λα.t), type application (t[T]), capture-avoiding type substitution, well-formedness checking, CBV evaluator with type erasure. Classic examples: polymorphic identity (∀α.α→α), polymorphic K (∀α.∀β.α→β→α), Church booleans (∀α.α→α→α), Church numerals (∀α.(α→α)→α→α), existential types via encoding, rank-2 types, parametricity/free theorems. 39 tests, 362 total.
blocks.details: System F (polymorphic lambda calculus): type variables, ∀-quantification, type abstraction (Λα.t), type application (t[T]), capture-avoiding type substitution, well-formedness checking, CBV evaluator with type erasure. Classic examples: polymorphic identity (∀α.α→α), polymorphic K (∀α.∀β.α→β→α), Church booleans (∀α.α→α→α), Church numerals (∀α.(α→α)→α→α), existential types via encoding, rank-2 types, parametricity/free theorems. 39 tests, 362 total.

blocks.task: System F stress tests: type-level computation, erasure, impredicativity, parametricity free theorems
blocks.summary: T14: System F (polymorphic lambda calculus): ∀-types, type abstraction/application,…
blocks.details: T14: System F (polymorphic lambda calculus): ∀-types, type abstraction/application, Church encodings in System F, existential types, rank-2 types, parametricity. 39 tests, 362 total.

blocks.task: Update lambda-calculus README with all 7 modules and project overview
blocks.summary: T16: Updated README with all modules + architecture diagram.
blocks.details: T16: Updated README with all modules + architecture diagram.

blocks.task: Build a Curry-Howard demo: propositions as types, proofs as programs in STLC/System F
blocks.summary: EXPLORE: Curry-Howard correspondence demo. 10 STLC theorems (identity, weakening, transitivity, conjunction intro/elim, commutativity, curry/uncurry, truth) + 3 System F theorems (polymorphic identity, weakening, transitivity) — all typechecked as proofs. Propositions-as-types translation, proofs-as-computations (running the proofs!), correspondence table. 40 new tests, 402 total across 8 modules.
blocks.details: EXPLORE: Curry-Howard correspondence demo. 10 STLC theorems (identity, weakening, transitivity, conjunction intro/elim, commutativity, curry/uncurry, truth) + 3 System F theorems (polymorphic identity, weakening, transitivity) — all typechecked as proofs. Propositions-as-types translation, proofs-as-computations (running the proofs!), correspondence table. 40 new tests, 402 total across 8 modules.

blocks.task: Update daily log, TASKS.md, final git push
blocks.summary: T17: Curry-Howard correspondence!
blocks.details: T17: Curry-Howard correspondence! Propositions as types, proofs as programs. 10 STLC + 3 System F theorems verified. 40 tests, 402 total across 8 modules.

blocks.task: Investigate neural net flaky test: loss decreases during training. Debug + fix.
blocks.summary: T19: Investigated neural net flaky test — project not found.
blocks.details: T19: Investigated neural net flaky test — project not found. Stale TODO item removed.

blocks.task: Extend type-inference: add let-rec inference tests, polymorphic higher-order functions, row polymorphism or type classes
blocks.summary: Extended type-inference with 42 advanced tests: polymorphic identity/const/flip/compose/apply, let-rec (factorial/fibonacci/GCD/power/isEven), higher-order (twice, church encoding, currying), advanced pairs/lists, operator edge cases, substitution chains, unification edge cases. FOUND BUG: resetFresh() + instantiate() causes infinite recursion when fresh variable names collide with scheme variable names in Subst.apply. 122 total type-inference tests.
blocks.details: Extended type-inference with 42 advanced tests: polymorphic identity/const/flip/compose/apply, let-rec (factorial/fibonacci/GCD/power/isEven), higher-order (twice, church encoding, currying), advanced pairs/lists, operator edge cases, substitution chains, unification edge cases. FOUND BUG: resetFresh() + instantiate() causes infinite recursion when fresh variable names collide with scheme variable names in Subst.apply. 122 total type-inference tests.

blocks.task: Session C final: update daily log, evening summary, git push

blocks.details: T20: Extended type-inference with 42 advanced HM tests. FOUND BUG: resetFresh+instantiate infinite recursion when fresh var names collide with scheme vars. 122 total type-inference tests.

blocks.task: Add proper node:test tests for regex engine: NFA, DFA, minimization, edge cases
blocks.summary: Regex engine: 66 new node:test tests covering basic matching, alternation, quantifiers (*/+/?/{n,m}), character classes ([abc], [a-z], [^x]), anchors, groups, escape characters (\d/\w/\s), NFA construction, DFA construction+matching, DFA minimization, complex patterns (email, IP, hex color), edge cases. Discovered engine does full-match (not substring) by default.
blocks.details: Regex engine: 66 new node:test tests covering basic matching, alternation, quantifiers (*/+/?/{n,m}), character classes ([abc], [a-z], [^x]), anchors, groups, escape characters (\d/\w/\s), NFA construction, DFA construction+matching, DFA minimization, complex patterns (email, IP, hex color), edge cases. Discovered engine does full-match (not substring) by default.

blocks.task: Lambda calculus: Normalization by Evaluation (NbE) module
blocks.summary: Normalization by Evaluation (NbE): semantic domain (VNeutral/VLam), evaluate into host closures, readback into normal-form syntax, beta-eta equality checking, Church numeral normalization (PLUS/MULT/PRED), NbE ≡ reduction equivalence proofs, performance test (100 nested identity apps). 29 new tests, 431 total lambda-calculus tests.
blocks.details: Normalization by Evaluation (NbE): semantic domain (VNeutral/VLam), evaluate into host closures, readback into normal-form syntax, beta-eta equality checking, Church numeral normalization (PLUS/MULT/PRED), NbE ≡ reduction equivalence proofs, performance test (100 nested identity apps). 29 new tests, 431 total lambda-calculus tests.

blocks.task: Add node:test tests for HenryDB: focus on window functions and stress cases
blocks.summary: HenryDB window functions: 15 new tests verifying ROW_NUMBER (with/without PARTITION BY), RANK and DENSE_RANK (with ties), aggregate window functions (SUM/AVG/COUNT/MIN/MAX OVER PARTITION BY), running totals (ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW), LAG/LEAD, FIRST_VALUE, NTILE. All window functions confirmed working correctly. Can remove TODO item.

blocks.task: Final exploration: add property-based tests for lambda calculus
blocks.summary: Property-based tests: alpha-equivalence reflexivity (20 random terms), NbE≡reduction agreement (9 terms), Church addition commutativity (25 pairs), Church addition associativity (64 triples), multiplication distributivity (27 triples), SKI compilation preservation (4 terms), Church-Rosser theorem (normal vs applicative), free variable preservation. 153 new tests, 584 total lambda-calculus tests.
blocks.details: Property-based tests: alpha-equivalence reflexivity (20 random terms), NbE≡reduction agreement (9 terms), Church addition commutativity (25 pairs), Church addition associativity (64 triples), multiplication distributivity (27 triples), SKI compilation preservation (4 terms), Church-Rosser theorem (normal vs applicative), free variable preservation. 153 new tests, 584 total lambda-calculus tests.

blocks.details: T32: De Bruijn reduction module. 25 tests, 609 total lambda-calculus tests.

queue.task: Review yesterday (1000 tests, lambda calculus, RISC-V backend). Assess bugs found (SMT conflicting bounds, type-inference resetFresh). Set direction.
queue.summary: Reviewed yesterday: 1000 tests, 2 bugs (SMT conflicting bounds, type-inference resetFresh). Queue is well-ordered — bugs first, then type checker integration, then lambda extensions. No queue modifications needed.

queue.goal: Fix 2 bugs found yesterday: SMT solver conflicting bounds + type-inference resetFresh collision
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks: fix SMT conflicting bounds in simplex, fix type-inference resetFresh collision. Context files identified.

queue.task: Fix SMT solver conflicting bounds bug — simplex assertBound/check not detecting infeasible bounds, add tests
queue.summary: Fixed both bugs. SMT: strict inequalities now adjust bounds (x<n→x<=n-1, x>n→x>=n+1), 6 new tests. Type-inference: freshVar uses t_ prefix to avoid collision, Subst.apply guards self-reference. 267 tests passing.

queue.task: Fix type-inference resetFresh collision — fresh var naming causes infinite recursion in instantiate, add tests
queue.summary: Already completed as part of T3 (both bugs fixed together).

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard check, email scan, GH notifications
queue.summary: Git pushed. Dashboard regenerated. GH notifications: 30+ neural-net CI failures (since Apr 12). No urgent emails.

queue.task: Reflect on bug fixes. Consider RISC-V GC vs monkey-lang type checker priority.
queue.summary: Reflections captured. Priority: monkey-lang type checker > RISC-V GC (bridges 2 projects). SMT bug lesson: strict→non-strict needs bound adjust for integers. Type-inf lesson: namespace fresh vars to avoid collision.

queue.goal: Monkey-lang type checker — connect type-inference project to monkey-lang compilation pipeline
queue.summary: Monkey-lang already has type annotations in AST (paramTypes, returnType). Need to build checker module using Algorithm W. Planned 2 BUILD tasks: core type checker + pipeline integration.

queue.task: Build monkey-lang type checker module: walk AST, infer types using Algorithm W, check annotations, report errors. Core types: int, bool, string, array, hash, fn. 20+ tests.
queue.summary: Built monkey-lang type checker: Algorithm W inference, parse type annotations (fn(x: int) -> int), 31 tests, all 1365+ existing tests pass. Added -> token to lexer, paramTypes/returnType in parser.

queue.task: Integrate type checker into compilation pipeline: run before compile/eval, flag --typecheck, type error formatting. 10+ integration tests.
queue.summary: Integrated type checker into REPL with --typecheck flag. Type errors reported before compilation. 14 integration tests. Backward compatible — annotations parsed but not enforced without flag.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard regen, knowledge capture
queue.summary: Git pushed all 4 repos. Dashboard regen (running). Knowledge system updated (algorithm-w scratch note). monkey-lang added as embedded repo warning — needs submodule setup later.

queue.task: Mid-day reflection. Depth check. Review type checker quality.
queue.summary: Depth check passed (surprises + learning). Type checker quality: 45 tests, missing loops/match/enum/modules. Decision: stress-test type checker before building new features (depth > breadth).

queue.goal: Lambda calculus extensions — dependent types or effect system
queue.summary: Planned CoC (Calculus of Constructions) as natural next step on lambda cube. 2 BUILD tasks: core CoC implementation + proof terms for Curry-Howard.

queue.task: Build Calculus of Constructions (CoC): dependent types, Pi types, type-level computation. Parser + evaluator + type checker with bidirectional typing. 30+ tests.
queue.summary: Built Calculus of Constructions: Pi types, dependent typing, bidirectional type checking, natElim, Church booleans, parser. 43 tests. Completes lambda cube: untyped→STLC→SystemF→CoC.

queue.task: Add proof terms for Curry-Howard with CoC: natural number proofs, vector length proofs, existential types. Connect to existing curry-howard module. 15+ tests.
queue.summary: CoC proof terms: Leibniz equality (refl, symm), sigma types, addition/double proofs, IsZero predicate, Vec type. Fixed natElim motive sort flexibility. 22 tests.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard regen, email check
queue.summary: Git pushed all repos. Monkey-lang and workspace pushed. Dashboard updates.

queue.task: Afternoon reflection. Evaluate lambda calculus extensions. Plan evening exploration.
queue.summary: 12 tasks complete. Depth check passed. Plan: stress-test CoC, then RISC-V GC if time. Quality strong.

queue.goal: Fix SMT solver conflicting bounds bug + extend SAT solver tests
queue.summary: Already completed in T3 (SMT strict inequality fix + tests).
Skipped — parent plan T14 already done in T3.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard regen
queue.summary: Already pushed in T12. Skipping duplicate maintain.

queue.task: Dependent type theory — CoC, LF, Idris/Agda type systems
queue.summary: Explored dependent type theory extensions. Key insight: inductive types are the most impactful next step — auto-generated eliminators for user-defined types. Also: universe hierarchy, pattern matching, termination checking.

queue.task: Research inline caching and hidden classes in V8 — compare with monkey-lang shapes
queue.summary: Monkey-lang already implements V8-style hidden classes + IC (mono/poly/mega). Transition caching exists. Missing: store ICs (only reads are cached). Architecture is solid.

queue.task: Stress-test monkey-lang type checker: edge cases, error paths, complex programs, find bugs
queue.summary: Stress-tested type checker: found 2 bugs (let-polymorphism missing — id used with int then string failed; negate-string silently passing). Fixed both. Added let-polymorphism with proper generalization. 51 unit + 14 integration = 65 tests.

queue.goal: PLAN: Design RISC-V garbage collector — root scanning, GC safe points, heap organization

queue.task: Add object headers to heap allocations (tag word: type tag + size). Needed for GC to walk the heap. Update alloc, string, array, closure, hash allocators. 15+ tests.
Implement Cheney semi-space copying GC: root scanning (registers + stack), copy-and-forward, pointer update. Trigger on alloc when half-space full. 20+ tests.
Stress-test GC: allocation-heavy programs, circular closures, long-lived vs short-lived objects. Fix bugs. 10+ tests.
